title: How to Choose Better Balsamic Vinegar

source.title: Arrêtez d'acheter du vinaigre balsamique au supermarché 🙅 #stop #cuisine #food #magasin

summary: This is a buying guide, not a recipe. The video argues that many supermarket "balsamic" products are either sweet glazes or thin vinegar blends, while a better balsamic should be dense, grape-must-forward, aged, and used sparingly as a finishing ingredient.
search_text: how to choose better balsamic vinegar how to choose better balsamic vinegar ingredient_guide arretez d'acheter du vinaigre balsamique au supermarche #stop #cuisine #food #magasin diego oggi pasta balsamic vinegar italian buying guide pantry balsamic vinegar italian buying guide pantry guide diet unknown italian balsamic vinegar pantry reference how to choose better balsamic vinegar summary this is a buying guide, not a recipe. the video argues that many supermarket "balsamic" products are either sweet glazes or thin vinegar blends, while a better balsamic should be dense, grape-must-forward, aged, and used sparingly as a finishing ingredient. key points avoid products where the character comes mainly from glucose syrup, caramel, flavoring, or a very sweet glaze texture. thin balsamic-style vinegar can contain grape must, but may also be stretched with wine vinegar; expect more acidity and less depth. better balsamic is visibly thicker and more concentrated. the key ingredient to look for is aged grape must. long-aged bottles can be expensive, but a younger dense option around 7 years can still be useful. use good balsamic as a finishing condiment rather than a bulk cooking acid. practical use check the ingredient list before buying. use a small amount on foods where the flavor will be noticeable: salads, cheese, vegetables, or simple finished plates. treat very sweet balsamic glazes as a different product from aged balsamic vinegar. source notes none for the current entry. source original title: arretez d'acheter du vinaigre balsamique au supermarche #stop #cuisine #food #magasin channel: diego oggi pasta url: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4jkxo3pisi

title: Vegetarian Soy Tofu Cheese

source.title: Un formage végé ?

summary: A simple firm tofu made by curdling fresh soy milk with diluted vinegar, then pressing the curds into a block. The creator frames it as a plant-based "formage" because the technique mirrors cheesemaking: curdle a protein-rich liquid, collect the curd, and give it a shape.
search_text: vegetarian soy tofu cheese vegetarian soy tofu cheese recipe un formage vege ? au bord de l'assiette tofu soy vegan plant-based cheese technique tofu soy vegan plant-based cheese technique snack vegan tofu soy cookable untested vegetarian soy tofu cheese summary a simple firm tofu made by curdling fresh soy milk with diluted vinegar, then pressing the curds into a block. the creator frames it as a plant-based "formage" because the technique mirrors cheesemaking: curdle a protein-rich liquid, collect the curd, and give it a shape. ingredients 500 g dried soybeans 6 l water, for blending the soaked soybeans 120 ml vinegar 120 ml water, for diluting the vinegar expected yield: about 6 l soy milk or about 500 g firm tofu. instructions soak the dried soybeans in a large volume of water for 8 hours. drain the soaked soybeans. blend the soaked soybeans in batches with 6 l water. line a sieve with cloth or a clean towel, then strain the blended mixture to obtain about 6 l soy milk. bring the soy milk to a simmer and cook it for 10 minutes. turn off the heat and let the milk cool for 5 minutes. mix 120 ml vinegar with 120 ml water, then pour the diluted vinegar into the hot soy milk. stir 3 to 4 times to distribute the acid, then leave the milk undisturbed for 10 minutes so it curdles. lift out the curds and transfer them to a perforated mold or faisselle lined with cloth. fold the cloth over the curds, place about 1.5 kg weight on top, and press for 30 minutes. remove the tofu from the cloth. it is ready to use. notes the source says vinegar works as the coagulant; comments discuss that other tofu coagulants may affect flavor differently. a strong blender makes the soy milk step easier. source notes none for the current entry. source original title: un formage vege ? channel: au bord de l'assiette url: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=putmmnqmzrw

title: Brian Lagerstrom GOAT Brownies

source.title: I Perfected This Brownie Recipe After 100 Tries

summary: A carefully developed brownie recipe using butter plus olive oil, white plus brown sugar, melted milk chocolate in the batter, Dutch cocoa, and chopped dark chocolate. The method emphasizes low heat, resting the batter, avoiding overmixing, and cooling fully before cutting.
search_text: brian lagerstrom goat brownies brian lagerstrom goat brownies recipe i perfected this brownie recipe after 100 tries brian lagerstrom brownies baking chocolate dessert brian lagerstrom brownies baking chocolate dessert brian lagerstrom dessert vegetarian chocolate baking cookable untested brian lagerstrom goat brownies summary a carefully developed brownie recipe using butter plus olive oil, white plus brown sugar, melted milk chocolate in the batter, dutch cocoa, and chopped dark chocolate. the method emphasizes low heat, resting the batter, avoiding overmixing, and cooling fully before cutting. ingredients 100 g all-purpose flour 1 g baking powder 4 g salt 30 g olive oil 115 g unsalted butter 150 g granulated sugar 50 g dark brown sugar 100 g milk chocolate chips 50 g dutch-processed cocoa powder, or 65 g regular cocoa powder 5 g vanilla extract 2 large eggs 85-100 g dark chocolate chips, chopped and sifted pan spray, for greasing instructions heat the oven to 163c without convection. grease a 20 cm square metal brownie pan with pan spray. in a small bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt. in a small saucepan over low heat, combine the olive oil, butter, granulated sugar, and dark brown sugar. stir until the butter is fully melted and the sugars are incorporated. the mixture will look sandy. remove from the heat and add the milk chocolate chips, stirring until melted. transfer to a medium bowl and cool for 3-5 minutes, until warm but not hot. whisk the dutch cocoa into the warm chocolate mixture until smooth and lump-free. stir in the vanilla and eggs, whisking thoroughly until fully combined. rest the mixture for 10 minutes so the sugar dissolves further. whisk again for 30-60 seconds until silky. add the dry ingredients and fold gently with a spatula until just combined. fold in the chopped dark chocolate chips. pour the batter into the prepared pan. do not spread with a spatula; firmly tap the pan on the counter several times to level the batter without disrupting the brownie skin. bake for 35 minutes. check doneness with an instant-read thermometer at about 95c, or with a cake tester that comes out with minimal sticky batter. cool in the pan for at least 60-90 minutes before cutting. notes the oil softens the crumb after cooling, while butter contributes flavor. brown sugar helps softness and flavor. milk chocolate rounds out bitterness; dark chocolate pieces give contrast. the source says freshly chopped dark chocolate can be sifted so chocolate dust does not muddy the batter. source notes none for current entry. source original title: i perfected this brownie recipe after 100 tries channel: brian lagerstrom url: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=al70j1h9x4
